div.h1: FIFO Buffer State: write-append + read-consume + memmove
div.p: M4 Semantics — append on write, consume-from-head on read, memmove remaining tail

g.text: Step 3 — write('CCCC', 4 bytes): append at buffer_data_len=3
Step 4 — Buffer Full: blocking write sleeps / O_NONBLOCK → -EAGAIN
M2/M3 vs M4 Buffer Semantics Comparison

div.p: buffer_data_len = 0 (no valid bytes) All bytes are FREE space • Legend: Blue=valid data Green=free
copy_from_user(kernel_buffer + buffer_data_len, buf, 6) // kernel_buffer + 0 ← append at tail buffer_data_len += 6 → buffer_data_len = 6 wake_up_interruptible(&mydevice_read_queue)
[0]=A [1]=A [2]=A [3]=B [4]=B [5]=B buffer_data_len = 6 ←▲ valid data ends here Appended at offset 0 (was empty). M4: does NOT overwrite — appends at tail.

to_copy = min(3, buffer_data_len=6) = 3 copy_to_user(userspace_buf, kernel_buffer, 3) // sends A,A,A buffer_data_len -= 3 → buffer_data_len = 3 memmove(kernel_buffer, kernel_buffer + 3, 3) // shift B,B,B to front wake_up_interruptible(&mydevice_write_queue) // space freed → wake writers

⚠️ memmove cost: O(remaining_bytes) — shifts buffer_data_len bytes Worst case: 4095 bytes shifted after 1-byte read ≈ 4–10 µs (64 cache lines) Circular buffer alternative: O(1) — advance tail index. Tradeoff: memmove = simpler code; circular = higher throughput.

space_available = buffer_size - buffer_data_len = 4096 - 3 = 4093 to_copy = min(4, 4093) = 4 copy_from_user(kernel_buffer + 3, buf, 4) // append C,C,C,C at offset 3 buffer_data_len += 4 → buffer_data_len = 7 wake_up_interruptible(&mydevice_read_queue)
[0]=B [1]=B [2]=B ← survived memmove [3]=C [4]=C [5]=C [6]=C ← newly appended CCCC buffer_data_len = 7 • FIFO order preserved
⛔ FULL: zero free bytes Writer cannot proceed until reader consumes bytes.
space = buffer_size - buffer_data_len; copy_from_user(kernel_buffer + buffer_data_len, buf, to_copy);
O(remaining_bytes) time Worst case: read 1, memmove 4094.
A,A,A = consumed (sent to user)\nB,B,B = remaining tail at [3..5]
B,B,B shifted to [0..2] via memmove\nbuffer_data_len = 3\nFree space at [3..4095]
